text.title: YAML Frontmatter Standards Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding YAML Frontmatter Standards. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Only keys from the published schema are allowed; unknown keys are rejected.
rule.rationale: Only keys from the published schema are allowed; unknown keys are rejected.

rule.statement: version field is semver MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH; non-semver rejected.
rule.rationale: version field is semver MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH; non-semver rejected.

rule.statement: last_reviewed is YYYY-MM-DD; other formats rejected.
rule.rationale: last_reviewed is YYYY-MM-DD; other formats rejected.

rule.statement: List fields use inline `[a, b]` syntax — the parser is line-based and does not handle block lists.
rule.rationale: List fields use inline `[a, b]` syntax — the parser is line-based and does not handle block lists.

rule.statement: Required keys must be non-empty and must not contain placeholder tokens like the three uppercase letters meaning 'to be done'.
rule.rationale: Required keys must be non-empty and must not contain placeholder tokens like the three uppercase letters meaning 'to be done'.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-yaml-frontmatter.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
